title: Selective automatic contact creation
excerpt: I want to be able to selectively create a contact when i send an email to someone new. Right now, either all my new emails will result in contact creation or none. Is there an option that I send an email to someone new, …

posts.author: butterfly (Yaser)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to be able to selectively create a contact when i send an email to someone new. Right now, either all my new emails will result in contact creation or none. Is there an option that I send an email to someone new, but also bcc a specific email address, and then only for those cases, contacts are created?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
I don’t believe so. That’s a great suggestion though! Maybe put it on the feature suggestion board? Feature requests - monday Community Forum

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yaser:

I want to be able to selectively create a contact when i send an email to someone new. Right now, either all my new emails will result in contact creation or none. Is there an option that I send an email to someone new, but also bcc a specific email address, and then only for those cases, contacts are created?

I found this advice online if it helps? Love you to let me know if it works!! Yes, monday.com does offer a way to selectively create contacts when sending emails—but it requires a specific setting adjustment in the Emails & Activities app.

Here’s how you can do it:

1. Use the BCC Method for Selective Logging

You can configure your Emails & Activities settings so that only emails BCC’d to your unique monday.com address are logged—and contacts are created only in those cases.

  • Go to Emails & Activities settings.
  • Under your connected email account, enable:
    • “Only log outgoing emails that have your Emails & Activities email address BCCed”
    • “Create a contact when you send an email to someone new” (optional toggle)

This way:

  • If you don’t BCC the special address, no contact is created.
  • If you do BCC it, the email is logged and a contact is created if the recipient is new

1

2

.

How to Find Your BCC Address

  • Open the Emails & Activities app on any item.
  • Click the gear icon () to open settings.
  • Your unique BCC address will be listed there.

Notes

  • This setting applies per email connection, so you can configure it differently for different users or shared inboxes.
  • You can also specify which board and email column new contacts should be created in

2

.

Would you like help locating your BCC address or setting up this configuration in your monday.com account?
